title: Luxuriantly British Butter Beans Sunday Roast
description: A rich and savory Sunday roast featuring butter beans, tender meat, and a silky kombu-cold-extract sauce, perfect for a special occasion or a cozy family gathering.

ingredients.name: kombu
ingredients.notes: reconstitute in water, then strain to get cold extract

instructions: Preheat oven to 300°F (150°C).
Season the beef brisket with salt and pepper.
Heat butter in a large dutch oven over medium-high heat.
Sear the brisket until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes per side.
Transfer the brisket to a plate and set aside.
Add the chopped thyme and rosemary to the pot and cook for 1 minute.
Add the flour and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Gradually add the beef broth, whisking constantly to avoid lumps.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce heat to a simmer.
Add the reconstituted kombu and cold extract to the pot, whisking to combine.
Add the browned brisket back to the pot and cover with a lid.
Transfer the pot to the preheated oven and roast for 2 1/2 hours, or until the meat is tender and falls apart easily.
While the brisket is roasting, cook the butter beans in a separate pot with a pinch of salt and pepper until tender, about 30 minutes.
Once the brisket is done, remove it from the pot and let it rest for 10 minutes before slicing.
Strain the juices from the pot and discard the solids.
Add the red wine to the pot (if using) and simmer until reduced by half, stirring occasionally.
Serve the sliced brisket with the butter beans and silky kombu-cold-extract sauce.
Garnish with fresh thyme and rosemary, if desired.
Serve immediately, hot and bubbly.
Reheat any leftovers in the oven or on the stovetop before serving.
Adjust seasoning to taste before serving, if necessary.
